posts.angle: Organizational transformation: scaling AI beyond pilots
posts.content:
Many AI pilots never make it out of the lab.

In our conversation with ING’s COO Risk, Leon Dusée, one theme stood out: the real challenge is not the model, but the organization around it.

If you want AI to scale, you need to treat it as an organizational transformation:
🔹 Decide early who owns the solution and how it will run in production
🔹 Focus on a handful of high‑impact use cases instead of experimenting everywhere
🔹 Invest in people, critical thinking, and adaptability just as much as in data and models

For Dutch organizations across banking, aviation, retail, education, and healthcare, this mindset is crucial. The biggest AI opportunities often come from redesigning end‑to‑end processes, not just optimizing individual tasks.

At KickstartAI, we see this daily. When leadership, governance, and clear problem ownership are in place, AI moves from “interesting pilot” to real impact for customers and society.

Curious about ING’s lessons from the field? Read the full interview with Leon Dusée here 👇
https://lnkd.in/eU8jqvT9

#AIadoption #AIinPractice #DigitalTransformation #KickstartAI

posts.angle: Focus over fragmentation in Dutch AI adoption
posts.content:
Across the Netherlands, organizations are running AI experiments in every corner of the business. The risk: a lot of activity, limited impact.

In his recent interview, ING COO Risk Leon Dusée shared a perspective we strongly recognize: scaling AI starts with focus, governance, and alignment.

For Dutch companies, that means:
✅ Choosing a limited number of high‑impact use cases and doing them really well
✅ Thinking about scaling, ownership, and risk from day one
✅ Making AI a joint effort between business, tech, and operations

This is not just about productivity. With the right focus, AI can strengthen financial health, improve education, and support better healthcare in the Netherlands.

At KickstartAI, we work with partners who are committed to this shift: fewer fragmented pilots, more shared learning and scalable solutions that benefit the wider ecosystem.

You can find ING’s lessons and concrete examples in the full piece with Leon Dusée here 👇
https://lnkd.in/eU8jqvT9

#AIstrategy #Netherlands #AIecosystem #KickstartAI

posts.angle: Collaboration and people as the core of AI impact
posts.content:
AI at scale is a team sport 🤝

Leon Dusée, COO Risk at ING, recently outlined why so many AI pilots stall: organizations underestimate how much AI changes processes, roles, and decision‑making.

To unlock AI’s potential for the Netherlands, we need to:
🔸 Prepare employees and leaders to work critically with AI, not just consume its output
🔸 Be willing to redesign entire processes, instead of automating single tasks
🔸 Collaborate across organizations so we can share lessons, avoid duplicate efforts, and accelerate responsible adoption

This is exactly where KickstartAI is focused: connecting partners in banking, aviation, retail, education, and healthcare to turn experimentation into production‑level impact that benefits society.

If you are working on similar challenges, ING’s experiences offer valuable guidance. Read the full interview with Leon Dusée here 👇
https://lnkd.in/eU8jqvT9

#ResponsibleAI #Collaboration #AIforSociety #KickstartAI

few_shot_examples.text:
AI is high on every agenda. But for many organizations, the question is where to start – and how to move beyond isolated initiatives.

In our latest article, we speak with Leon Dusée, Global Head COO Risk at ING about how to apply AI in practice. He explains that successful AI adoption requires focus, clear choices, and early thinking about how solutions will scale within the organization.

In the interview, Dusée shares lessons on:
🔸 Why AI pilots often stall
🔸 What leaders need to take into account when implementing AI solutions
🔸 The importance of investing in people alongside technology
🔸 And how AI can contribute to broader societal impact

Read the full article below 👇 
https://lnkd.in/eU8jqvT9

Very proud to see the CEOs of our founding partners featured in this insightful article by Het Financieele Dagblad!

📌 In this article, Frans Muller (Ahold Delhaize), Steven van Rijswijk (ING), Wouter Koolmees (Nederlandse Spoorwegen), and Marjan Rintel (KLM Royal Dutch Airlines) shared their lessons from deploying AI in large organizations: from reducing food waste and detecting unattended objects at train stations to fighting money laundering and predicting actual passenger numbers to better plan onboard services. 

What makes this piece special to us isn’t just the mention of KickstartAI, but the shared philosophy reflected: 

🔹 Focus over fragmentation
🔹 Start with the problem, not the technology
🔹 Don’t force AI on people, make it work for them
🔹 Combine operational reality with strategic vision
🔹 Move from isolated experiments to scalable practices

We’re proud of our partners, their honesty about what works (and what doesn’t), and how they keep pushing for AI that actually serves people, processes, and society.

👉 This is a must-read for any company navigating the shift from AI potentials to real operational impact. 

Read the full article below:
https://lnkd.in/erNQm2xt

#AI #AholdDelhaize #ING #NS #KLM #KickstartAI

AI holds a €85bn potential for the Dutch economy, but as Sophia Claire Zitman pointed out at TNW Conference, adoption is lagging.

It’s not about the technology, talent, or compute. It’s that AI is still stuck in the "potential" phase — too abstract, too big, too misaligned with how organizations actually work.

To unlock its impact, we have to stop treating AI like another IT upgrade. We have to rethink how we work and operate. We need to move from traditional metrics like OKRs and KPIs, to focusing on the people within organizations who are ready to reimagine what’s possible — and act on it.

In our projects, this approach has led to real results:
✈️  KLM Royal Dutch Airlines cut food waste by 65%
🛍️ bol improved parcel delivery efficiency by 6%
🚉 Nederlandse Spoorwegen train stations increasing safety 

👉 The potential is clear. The time to move from exploration to execution is now.

#KicstartAI #AIinNetherlands #AIadoption
